name: Mechanism deriving the Koide relation Q = 2/3
closability_note: The Koide formula Q = (m_e + m_mu + m_tau) / (sqrt(m_e) + sqrt(m_mu) + sqrt(m_tau))^2 = 2/3 holds to 5 decimal places in the charged-lepton sector, but no convincing derivation exists. Could plausibly be a numerical coincidence rather than a deep relation — the running of charged-lepton masses from EW to high scales spoils the formula, so any 'derivation' must explain why it holds AT the EW scale specifically. Modular flavor symmetries at level 3 are the most active research direction; Koide's own democratic-mass model gives Q exactly but introduces unmotivated input choices. The gap is open in the strict sense, contested in whether closure is even desirable (vs accepting the formula as coincidence).
summary: The Koide formula relating charged-lepton masses holds to ~5 sigma precision at the EW scale but has no derivation. No framework in the catalog claims to predict Q = 2/3.
what_closes_it: A mechanism that derives Q = 2/3 (or whatever value the formula actually takes at the relevant scale) from a smaller set of structural inputs. Candidate routes: (a) modular flavor symmetry at level N=3 producing charged-lepton mass matrices whose eigenvalues satisfy the Koide constraint as a property of the modular forms (Koide-Yukawa relations from group theory; Feruglio-Romanino 2021 explored this direction); (b) a symmetry-protected mass texture that gives the formula as a fixed point of the RG flow; (c) a UV embedding where the formula reflects an underlying SU(3) democratic mass structure broken in a specific direction. None of these has succeeded; the formula's behaviour under running (it breaks at scales above ~1 TeV) is a significant obstacle to any derivation that invokes a high-scale structure.
scale_hint: Low scale (~ 100 GeV - 1 TeV). The formula holds at the EW scale and breaks under RG running, so the closing mechanism must operate near the IR or be RG-protected.

principles_at_stake.note: If the formula is genuinely an accident, then 'naturalness' doesn't demand explanation. If it's a structural prediction, then any derivation that doesn't survive RG running needs an explanation for why the IR is special.

literature_anchors.citation: Koide, 'A fermion-boson composite model of quarks and leptons', PLB 120, 161 (1983)
Foot, 'A note on Koide's lepton mass relation' (1994)
Sumino, 'Family gauge symmetry as an origin of Koide's mass formula and charged lepton spectrum', JHEP 0905 (2009) 075
